articles.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
articles.summary: Research from the Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace report (surveying 500 security leaders) identifies secure data movement as a critical gap in Zero Trust implementations. The article argues that simply connecting systems and pushing data through gateways does not solve the underlying security problem, causing Zero Trust programs to stall.

articles.title: Critical Unpatched Flaw Leaves Hugging Face LeRobot Open to Unauthenticated RCE
articles.summary: CVE-2026-25874 (CVSS 9.3) in Hugging Face's LeRobot robotics platform stems from untrusted data deserialization, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. The flaw affects the open-source project with ~24,000 GitHub stars and remains unpatched at disclosure.

articles.title: After Mythos: New Playbooks For a Zero-Window Era
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude Mythos and Project Glasswing demonstrate that exploit windows are collapsing as AI can now rapidly identify and weaponize vulnerabilities post-disclosure. Network detection and response (NDR) becomes critical as patching timelines compress in this new threat landscape.

articles.title: An update on GitHub availability
articles.summary: GitHub announced a capacity scaling plan from 10X to 30X current infrastructure driven by exponential growth in agentic development workflows since December 2025. Repository creation, pull requests, API usage, and large-repository workloads are growing rapidly, requiring architectural redesign for availability and efficiency.

articles.title: Chinese Silk Typhoon Hacker Extradited to U.S. Over COVID Research Cyberattacks
articles.summary: Xu Zewei, 34, a member of the Chinese state-sponsored Silk Typhoon group, was extradited from Italy to face charges for cyberattacks against U.S. organizations and government agencies between February 2020 and June 2021, including targeting COVID research.

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
articles.summary: Microsoft Entra ID's Agent ID Administrator role, introduced for AI agent identity lifecycle management, contained a privilege escalation flaw enabling service principal and identity takeover. Silverfort researchers disclosed the vulnerability, which Microsoft has now patched.

articles.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
articles.summary: CVE-2026-32202 (CVSS 4.3), a Windows Shell spoofing vulnerability patched in Patch Tuesday, is now confirmed under active exploitation in the wild. The flaw allows attackers to access sensitive information through shell interaction.

articles.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS
articles.summary: GPT models, Codex, and Managed Agents are now available directly on AWS, allowing enterprises to run OpenAI capabilities within their AWS environments for compliance and security. This integration enables private deployments without routing requests through OpenAI's infrastructure.

articles.title: Our commitment to community safety
articles.summary: OpenAI describes its safety mechanisms for ChatGPT including model safeguards, misuse detection, and policy enforcement. The approach combines technical controls with collaboration with external safety experts.

articles.title: How leading tech companies are killing the builder's tax with Lakebase
articles.summary: Databricks positions Lakebase as a solution to reduce operational overhead in AI app development. The post argues that hidden infrastructure costs compound across data pipelines, governance, and model serving, and that unified data platforms can lower total cost of ownership for AI-native applications.

articles.title: Malicious Release of elementary-data PyPI Package Steals Cloud Credentials from Data Engineers
articles.summary: Attackers exploited a GitHub Actions script injection vulnerability to publish elementary-data v0.23.3 with an embedded credential stealer targeting dbt profiles, cloud provider keys, and SSH secrets from data engineering environments. The attack demonstrates supply-chain risk in data tooling.

articles.title: Microsoft-OpenAI rewrite opens the door for Anthropic and Google
articles.summary: OpenAI and Microsoft restructured their partnership agreement, allowing OpenAI to deliver services across cloud platforms beyond Azure while maintaining revenue-share payments to Microsoft through 2030. The change signals OpenAI's intent to broaden enterprise reach across multiple cloud hyperscalers and Microsoft's parallel push to develop its own AI models and expand Microsoft 365 Copilot offerings.

articles.title: GitHub moves Copilot to usage-based billing as AI coding costs climb
articles.summary: GitHub is shifting Copilot from fixed monthly fees with premium request units to a credit-based model tied to token consumption, effective June 1. Each plan includes a monthly allotment of GitHub AI credits with overage charges, aligning pricing with actual usage as the company manages demand and seeks a sustainable business model.

articles.title: Why AI engineering needs old-school discipline
articles.summary: Thoughtworks data and AI advisor Nimisha Asthagiri discusses why 40 percent of agentic AI projects are predicted to be canceled by end of 2027, citing a PoC-to-production gap. She argues that engineering fundamentals like test-driven development and mutation testing are making a comeback, and that executives should ask what new capabilities they can build rather than just how to move faster.

articles.title: Kubernetes v1.36: Mutable Pod Resources for Suspended Jobs (beta)
articles.summary: Kubernetes v1.36 promotes mutable pod resources for suspended Jobs to beta, allowing queue controllers and administrators to adjust CPU, memory, GPU, and extended resource specs in a Job's pod template while suspended. This eliminates the need to delete and recreate jobs when resource requirements change, preserving metadata and history while enabling dynamic allocation based on cluster capacity and hardware availability.

articles.title: Automate repetitive tasks with Amazon Quick Flows
articles.summary: Amazon Quick Flows enables natural language workflow automation without coding, turning manual data tasks into AI-powered processes. Part of the Amazon Quick suite, it lets users describe repetitive workflows (data copying, formatting, reporting) and automatically builds them using conversational AI, targeting individual and team productivity.

articles.title: Build and deploy an automatic sync solution for Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases
articles.summary: An event-driven serverless architecture automatically synchronizes S3 documents to Bedrock Knowledge Bases while respecting API quotas and rate limits. The solution detects S3 events, orchestrates ingestion jobs, and provides monitoring to keep knowledge bases current for real-time agent responses without manual intervention.

articles.title: Build Strands Agents with SageMaker AI models and MLflow
articles.summary: Strands Agents SDK integrates with SageMaker AI endpoints to build agents with organizational control over compute, scaling, and infrastructure placement. The post demonstrates deploying JumpStart models on SageMaker endpoints, integrating with Strands Agents, implementing MLflow observability for agent tracing, and A/B testing model variants.

articles.title: How Popsa used Amazon Nova to inspire customers with personalised title suggestions
articles.summary: Popsa integrated Amazon Nova Lite and Pro with Claude 3 Haiku via Bedrock to auto-generate creative photo book titles and subtitles across 12 languages using metadata, computer vision, and RAG. The unified Bedrock API improved quality, reduced latency, and cut costs while maintaining brand alignment.

articles.title: "I was tired of explaining it to somebody who was supposed to build it for me": Meet the executives vibe-coding their own tools.
articles.summary: Executives and C-suite leaders are using Claude, Cursor, and embedded AI features to build production agents and automation tools without traditional developers. Moshe Bar, CEO of Codenotary, built a 140,000-line BBS system for IBM 3270 mainframe terminals using Claude, personally editing only 10 lines, with 500 users and zero downtime.

articles.title: GitHub Copilot is moving to usage-based billing
articles.summary: GitHub Copilot plans transition to usage-based billing on June 1, 2026, replacing premium-request counting with monthly GitHub AI Credits allotments priced by token consumption (input, output, cached). The shift reflects Copilot's evolution into an agentic platform with multi-step autonomous coding sessions that demand significantly higher compute and inference resources than the original flat-rate model could sustain.

articles.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: Anthropic & Meta partnership, AWS Lambda S3 Files, Amazon Bedrock AgentCore CLI, and more (April 27, 2026)
articles.summary: AWS and Anthropic deepened collaboration with Claude training on AWS Trainium and Graviton silicon, co-engineered for computational efficiency. Claude Cowork is now available in Amazon Bedrock, enabling teams to collaborate with Claude as an agentic partner while keeping data within AWS infrastructure.

articles.title: What the 2026 State of Open Source report reveals about digital autonomy
articles.summary: The 2026 State of Open Source Report from Perforce OpenLogic shows that 55 percent of respondents cite avoiding vendor lock-in as a primary driver of open source adoption, up 68 percent year-over-year, with 63 percent in Europe. Organizations are increasingly viewing open source as a mechanism for control and long-term leverage as licensing models and regulatory mandates shift.

articles.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
articles.summary: Checkmarx's investigation into its March 23, 2026 supply-chain attack reveals that threat actors accessed and exfiltrated data from the company's GitHub repository, later posting it on the dark web. The initial compromise was facilitated through the supply-chain attack vector.

articles.title: Why developers are betting on Postgres for AI
articles.summary: Postgres is emerging as the preferred database for AI applications because structured data serves as ground truth to prevent LLM hallucination. 66% of Stack Overflow 2025 survey respondents use or want to use Postgres, making it the leading choice for enterprises building AI agents that need reliable access to existing customer records and domain data.

articles.title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
articles.summary: ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API now meet FedRAMP Moderate authorization standards, enabling U.S. federal agencies to adopt OpenAI models with compliance assurance. This certification removes a major barrier for government deployment.

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
articles.summary: Weekly roundup covering Fast16 malware, supply chain incidents, fake help desk attacks, malicious browser extensions, credential theft, and abuse of remote tools. The recap highlights recurring attack patterns and legacy vulnerabilities still being exploited.

articles.title: Can your operations handle your security ambitions?
articles.summary: Enterprise security teams often struggle to operationalize new security tools because legacy systems, manual workflows, and misalignment between security and engineering stacks create friction. The article warns that adding sophisticated security tooling without addressing underlying operational complexity can turn security into a bottleneck rather than an enabler.

articles.title: GitHub veteran Brian Douglas launches Paper Compute to fix AI agent infrastructure
articles.summary: Paper Compute is building open-source cloud-native infrastructure for deploying and managing AI agents in production. The startup addresses the gap between agent development and production operations, providing tooling for confidence and control across diverse deployment scenarios.

articles.title: Mythos Changed the Math on Vulnerability Discovery. Most Teams Aren't Ready for the Remediation Side
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview (announced April 7) can identify vulnerabilities at scale, forcing organizations to rethink validation, prioritization, and remediation workflows. The article examines the gap between vulnerability discovery acceleration and remediation readiness.

articles.title: PhantomCore Exploits TrueConf Vulnerabilities to Breach Russian Networks
articles.summary: Pro-Ukrainian hacktivist group PhantomCore has been actively targeting TrueConf video conferencing servers in Russia since September 2025 using an exploit chain of three vulnerabilities to achieve remote code execution. Positive Technologies documented the campaign, detailing how the threat actors chain multiple CVEs in the conferencing software to compromise susceptible instances.

articles.title: Researchers Uncover 73 Fake VS Code Extensions Delivering GlassWorm v2 Malware
articles.summary: Researchers identified 73 malicious VS Code extensions on the Open VSX repository cloned from legitimate counterparts as part of the GlassWorm information-stealing campaign, with six confirmed as actively malicious. The attack leverages the trust developers place in extension repositories to distribute infostealer payloads.

articles.title: Kubernetes for platform teams: Leveraging k0s and k0rdent
articles.summary: Explores using k0s, k0rdent, and Hosted Control Planes to manage multi-cluster Kubernetes deployments on OpenStack at scale. Addresses the overhead problem of running dozens or hundreds of clusters by consolidating control planes, reducing per-cluster node requirements from 3+ dedicated control-plane nodes to shared infrastructure.

articles.title: Inside one of the first production deployments of Lakebase: LangGuard's agentic workflow governance engine
articles.summary: Databricks showcases LangGuard's use of Lakebase to build governance controls for autonomous AI agents. The case study highlights how Lakebase enables monitoring, auditing, and policy enforcement for multi-step agentic workflows at scale.

articles.title: Fake CAPTCHA IRSF Scam and 120 Keitaro Campaigns Drive Global SMS, Crypto Fraud
articles.summary: Researchers uncovered a telecommunications fraud operation using fake CAPTCHA verification to trick users into sending international SMS messages that generate charges on their mobile bills, with 120 Keitaro campaigns identified as part of the scheme. The threat actors monetize by leasing phone numbers and collecting the fraudulent SMS charges.

articles.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
articles.summary: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended partnership agreement that clarifies long-term commitments and governance. The deal simplifies the relationship while supporting continued AI infrastructure investment and innovation.

articles.title: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
articles.summary: Symphony is an open-source specification for orchestrating Codex agents that converts issue trackers into autonomous agent systems. The spec enables engineers to reduce context switching and automate task routing by treating tickets as persistent agent inputs.

articles.title: Qinglong task scheduler RCE vulnerabilities exploited in the wild for cryptomining
articles.summary: Two authentication bypass flaws (CVE-2026-3965, CVE-2026-4047) in Qinglong's task scheduler were actively exploited to deploy cryptomining malware on self-hosted instances. The post covers attack mechanics and defensive lessons for operators of similar scheduling tools.

articles.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
articles.summary: Tutorial on implementing OpenAI's Privacy Filter to build privacy-preserving web applications that integrate LLM APIs while protecting user data.

articles.title: Introducing the New Agentic Architecture for Snyk Agent Fix: Faster, Smarter, and More Secure
articles.summary: Snyk Agent Fix now uses an agentic architecture for AI-driven code remediation with full Snyk Code language coverage and verified fixes. The upgrade enables faster and more reliable automated vulnerability patching across supported languages.

articles.title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
articles.summary: Choco deployed OpenAI APIs to build agent systems that streamline food distribution logistics and reduce manual coordination overhead. The case study demonstrates measurable productivity gains from agentic workflows in supply-chain operations.

articles.title: The next generation of Databricks Genie
articles.summary: Databricks announces an updated Genie product with improved natural language-to-SQL capabilities, faster query generation, and enhanced reasoning over complex schemas. The new version targets both analysts and data engineers for self-service analytics.

articles.title: The disappearing AI middle class
articles.summary: The LLM market is bifurcating into expensive frontier models (OpenAI's GPT-5.5 doubled pricing) and cheap open alternatives (DeepSeek), eliminating the comfortable middle tier. Developers building agents and inference pipelines must now choose between premium closed models or budget open infrastructure, complicating cost-performance optimization.

articles.title: Beyond prompting: How KubeStellar reached 81% PR acceptance with AI agents
articles.summary: Building KubeStellar Console with AI agents revealed that codebase maturity, not model capability, is the limiting factor for sustained productivity. Initial rapid gains gave way to cascading failures and architectural drift; success required strong test coverage, clear patterns, and disciplined code organization to keep agents productive.

articles.title: Model Risk Management in 2026: A Banker's Guide to the Revised Interagency Guidance
articles.summary: Databricks provides guidance on updated Federal Reserve model risk management requirements effective April 2026. The post contextualizes regulatory changes for financial institutions deploying AI and ML models.

articles.title: Build with DeepSeek V4 Using NVIDIA Blackwell and GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4-Pro (1.6T parameters, 49B active) and DeepSeek-V4-Flash (284B parameters, 13B active) enable efficient million-token context inference on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. The post covers deployment patterns for both flagship and speed-optimized variants.

articles.title: Kubernetes v1.36: Fine-Grained Kubelet API Authorization Graduates to GA
articles.summary: Fine-grained kubelet API authorization reaches GA in v1.36, replacing the overly broad nodes/proxy permission with granular RBAC controls over kubelet HTTPS endpoints. The feature gate KubeletFineGrainedAuthz is now locked to enabled, allowing monitoring and observability tools to access metrics and logs without granting the ability to execute commands in containers.

articles.title: Building Workforce AI Agents with Visier and Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Amazon Quick, an agentic AI workspace, integrates with Visier's workforce intelligence platform via Model Context Protocol (MCP) to give knowledge workers unified access to live HR data and organizational context. Intelligent agents retrieve and reason across enterprise knowledge, business intelligence, and workflow automation simultaneously, enabling workforce decisions grounded in real data without tool-switching.

articles.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
articles.summary: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without requiring major code refactoring, addressing the practical constraint that valuable data is often immovable due to regulatory boundaries and data sovereignty rules. The framework handles distributed model training across data silos while managing data gravity and compliance friction.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 achieves 1M token context window with practical performance for agentic applications, addressing the gap between theoretical and usable long-context capabilities.

articles.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI–Assisted Coding
articles.summary: Three LLM agents generated over 600,000 lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win a Kaggle competition, demonstrating how agents combined with GPU acceleration compress the experiment-iteration loop in ML competitions. The approach shows practical value of agentic workflows for rapid prototyping and testing at scale.

articles.title: Kubernetes v1.36: User Namespaces in Kubernetes are finally GA
articles.summary: User Namespaces support reaches GA in Kubernetes v1.36 (Linux-only), enabling rootless security isolation for workloads. Setting hostUsers: false namespaces capabilities like CAP_NET_ADMIN, allowing containers to run with privileges confined to the user namespace without affecting the host, preventing privilege escalation via kernel vulnerabilities or mount misconfigurations.

articles.title: Amazon Quick for marketing: From scattered data to strategic action
articles.summary: Amazon Quick connects fragmented marketing systems and data sources into a personal knowledge graph that learns user priorities and preferences. The agentic workspace answers questions grounded in real business data and executes tasks, reducing manual data assembly from hours to minutes for campaign performance analysis.

articles.title: Applying multimodal biological foundation models across therapeutics and patient care
articles.summary: AWS provides a unified environment for multimodal biological foundation models (BioFMs) that integrate fragmented healthcare data streams including omics, medical images, clinical trials, and EHRs. BioFMs pre-trained on large biological datasets enable drug discovery and personalized medicine workflows by surfacing insights hidden in relationships between data types.

articles.title: Trivy, KICS, and the shape of supply chain attacks so far in 2026
articles.summary: Docker documented two supply-chain compromises (Trivy and Checkmarx KICS) where stolen publisher credentials pushed malicious images to Docker Hub. The KICS attack overwrote five existing tags and created two new ones, with the poisoned binary exfiltrating scan output to attacker infrastructure while maintaining legitimate scanning surface.

articles.title: From Ingress NGINX to Higress: migrating 60+ resources in 30 minutes with AI
articles.summary: Demonstrates using an AI agent to automate migration of 60+ Ingress NGINX resources to Higress, an AI-native API gateway built on Envoy and Istio. Higress provides LLM-specific features including token-based rate limiting, prompt caching, and unified LLM provider protocols, completing validation in 30 minutes versus months of manual refactoring.

articles.title: Introducing GPT-5.5
articles.summary: GPT-5.5 is OpenAI's latest model, offering improved speed and capability for complex tasks including coding, research, and multi-tool data analysis. The release emphasizes performance gains over the prior generation without detailed benchmarks or architectural changes disclosed.

articles.title: GPT-5.5 System Card
articles.summary: OpenAI's system card for GPT-5.5 documents model capabilities, limitations, and safety considerations. The card provides technical details on the model's performance characteristics and intended use cases.

articles.title: Plugins and skills
articles.summary: Guide to extending Codex with plugins and skills for tool integration, data access, and repeatable workflows. Demonstrates how to connect external systems and automate complex multi-step tasks.

articles.title: What is Codex?
articles.summary: Codex is OpenAI's agent framework that moves beyond chat to automate tasks, connect external tools, and produce structured outputs like documents and dashboards. The guide covers how Codex enables tool-use and workflow automation.

articles.title: Automations
articles.summary: Guide to scheduling and triggering automated tasks in Codex for recurring workflows like report generation and summarization. Covers how to eliminate manual execution overhead.

articles.title: Top 10 uses for Codex at work
articles.summary: Catalog of 10 practical Codex applications including task automation, deliverable generation, and cross-tool workflows. Provides concrete examples of agent-driven productivity patterns.

articles.title: How to get started with Codex
articles.summary: Step-by-step guide to setting up Codex projects, creating threads, and executing first tasks. Covers foundational setup for users new to the agent platform.

articles.title: Working with Codex
articles.summary: Comprehensive guide to Codex workspace setup, thread and project creation, file management, and task execution. Covers core operational patterns for agent-driven workflows.

articles.title: Codex settings
articles.summary: Configuration guide for Codex covering personalization options, detail level controls, and permission management. Enables users to customize agent behavior and workflow constraints.

articles.title: Bridging the trust gap: Unified public CA orchestration with IBM Vault
articles.summary: IBM Vault Enterprise now integrates and orchestrates public certificate authorities directly within Vault, enabling automated workflows for both internal and externally-trusted certificates. This eliminates manual certificate management across external CA portals and provides centralized visibility of expiration dates and renewal timelines.

articles.title: GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty
articles.summary: Red-teaming challenge for GPT-5.5 focused on discovering universal jailbreaks related to biosafety risks. Offers up to $25,000 in rewards for finding model vulnerabilities in biological domains.

articles.title: How to Use Transformers.js in a Chrome Extension
articles.summary: Guide to running transformer models locally in Chrome extensions using Transformers.js, enabling on-device inference without server calls.

articles.title: Simplify Sparse Deep Learning with Universal Sparse Tensor in nvmath-python
articles.summary: Universal Sparse Tensor (UST) integration into nvmath-python v0.9.0 decouples tensor sparsity from memory layout, enabling developers to optimize sparse scientific and deep learning workloads with greater flexibility. The feature targets performance improvements for sparse tensor operations without layout-specific constraints.

articles.title: Cost-effective multilingual audio transcription at scale with Parakeet-TDT and AWS Batch
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Parakeet-TDT-0.6B-v3 model, deployed on AWS Batch with GPU instances and EC2 Spot, achieves inference speeds orders of magnitude faster than real-time by using Token-and-Duration Transducer architecture to skip silence. The open-source multilingual ASR model supports 25 European languages with automatic detection, reducing transcription costs to fractions of a cent per audio hour.

articles.title: Scaling the AI-Ready Data Center with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition and NVIDIA vGPU 20
articles.summary: NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition and vGPU 20 enable virtualized GPU access for enterprise applications integrating AI workloads. The solution addresses GPU compute bottlenecks by providing secure, multi-tenant access to dedicated GPU resources across data centers.

articles.title: Advancing Emerging Optimizers for Accelerated LLM Training with NVIDIA Megatron
articles.summary: NVIDIA Megatron now supports higher-order optimization algorithms like Shampoo and Muon for LLM training. Muon was used to train Kimi K2 and GLM-5, demonstrating practical speedups for large-scale model training workflows.

articles.title: Amazon SageMaker AI now supports optimized generative AI inference recommendations
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI now integrates NVIDIA AIPerf benchmarking to deliver validated, optimal GPU configurations and deployment recommendations with performance metrics, eliminating weeks of manual testing. The integration with NVIDIA Dynamo's distributed inference framework enables developers to move from model development to production deployment faster.

articles.title: Get to your first working agent in minutes: Announcing new features in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore abstracts away agent infrastructure (compute, sandboxing, tool connections, storage, error recovery) to let developers focus on agent logic using frameworks like LangGraph, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, and Strands Agents. New capabilities streamline the path from prototype to production by eliminating the need to build orchestration layers and harnesses from scratch.

articles.title: Faster threat detection with Boundary session recording + Auditbeat
articles.summary: HashiCorp Boundary session recordings combined with Elastic Auditbeat kernel-level audit events create structured event streams for SIEM ingestion, enabling detection rules and automated alerts for privileged access monitoring. This approach satisfies regulated industries (SOX, DORA, PCI DSS, HIPAA) by pairing forensic video detail with queryable event data for SOC workflows.

articles.title: SELinux Volume Label Changes goes GA (and likely implications in v1.37)
articles.summary: Kubernetes v1.36 finalizes SELinuxMount feature support; a future release (anticipated v1.37) will enable it by default, switching from recursive relabeling to faster ID-mapped mount labeling. This breaks applications sharing volumes between privileged and unprivileged pods on the same node, requiring audits and opt-outs in v1.36 for SELinux-enforcing clusters.

articles.title: It's all about the angle: Your photos, re-composed
articles.summary: Google Research presents a generative model that automatically recomposes photographs by adjusting framing and perspective. The system learns to improve composition through learned aesthetic principles.

articles.title: Terraform adds pre-written Sentinel policies for ISO 27001
articles.summary: HashiCorp and AWS co-created pre-written Sentinel policies for AWS that map to ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A controls, covering access control, cryptography, logging, and secure configuration. These policies are available in the Terraform Registry and lower the barrier to adopting policy-as-code for compliance without requiring organizations to translate frameworks from scratch.

articles.title: Company-wise memory in Amazon Bedrock with Amazon Neptune and Mem0
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now supports company-wise memory via Amazon Neptune and Mem0, enabling AI agents to maintain persistent, organization-specific context across multiple interactions. TrendMicro's Companion chatbot demonstrates the pattern: combining long-term organizational knowledge with short-term conversation history while ensuring memory accuracy and security at enterprise scale.

articles.title: Making ChatGPT better for clinicians
articles.summary: OpenAI launches free ChatGPT for Clinicians for verified U.S. physicians, nurse practitioners, and pharmacists. Targets clinical documentation, care support, and research workflows.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo presents a new distributed training algorithm for resilient large-scale model training. Addresses fault tolerance and communication efficiency in distributed AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
articles.summary: Technical deep dive into Codex agent loop optimization using WebSockets and connection-scoped caching to reduce API overhead and improve model latency. Concrete performance improvements in the Responses API.

articles.title: From zero trust to continuous trust: Securing autonomous AI systems
articles.summary: HashiCorp argues that traditional zero-trust security models break down with agentic AI systems, which operate continuously and request access dynamically rather than following discrete authentication checkpoints. The post proposes 'continuous trust' as an evolution that accounts for agents making real-time decisions, calling APIs, and generating credentials as part of task completion, requiring new security assumptions beyond verify-at-entry models.

articles.title: Kubernetes v1.36: ハル (Haru)
articles.summary: Kubernetes v1.36 ships 70 enhancements: 18 graduating to Stable, 25 entering Beta, and 25 to Alpha. The release includes deprecations and removals; check the full notes for migration guidance on affected APIs.

articles.title: From developer desks to the whole organization: Running Claude Cowork in Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Claude Cowork is now available in Amazon Bedrock, bringing multi-step research, document analysis, file processing, and report generation to knowledge workers via a desktop application. Data remains under customer control with no storage of prompts, files, or model responses; Anthropic-hosted features like Computer Use are available separately.

articles.title: End-to-end lineage with DVC and Amazon SageMaker AI MLflow apps
articles.summary: AWS demonstrates integrating DVC, SageMaker, and SageMaker MLflow Apps to provide full model lineage traceability from training data through deployment. The solution supports dataset-level and record-level lineage patterns, critical for regulated industries like healthcare and financial services where audit trails must link deployed models to exact training data and individual records.

articles.title: ReasoningBank: Enabling agents to learn from experience
articles.summary: Google introduces ReasoningBank, a framework that allows AI agents to accumulate and learn from past reasoning traces and experiences. The system enables agents to improve decision-making over time by storing and retrieving relevant reasoning patterns from previous tasks.

articles.title: Gateway API v1.5: Moving features to Stable
articles.summary: Gateway API v1.5 promotes six Experimental features to Stable: ListenerSet, TLSRoute, HTTPRoute CORS Filter, Client Certificate Validation, Certificate Selection for Gateway, and TLS Origination. The project adopts a release-train model with feature freeze dates and new Release Manager roles.

articles.title: From 80 days to 5: How Banco Bradesco accelerated digital product delivery with HCP Terraform
articles.summary: Banco Bradesco reduced infrastructure provisioning time from 80 days to 5 days by positioning HCP Terraform as a control plane for platform engineering, replacing fragmented automation with governed orchestration across 20+ internal teams and 500+ modules. The shift unified change management, policy validation, and CMDB registration into a self-service model, enabling faster time-to-market for digital products.

articles.title: Auto-diagnosing Kubernetes alerts with HolmesGPT and CNCF tools
articles.summary: STCLab's SRE team built an alert investigation pipeline using HolmesGPT (ReAct pattern) to automatically diagnose Kubernetes alerts across EKS clusters. The LLM agent chains tools (Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, kubectl) based on findings rather than fixed runbooks, reducing manual triage from 15-20 minutes to automatic first-pass analysis in Slack.

articles.title: 'Scattered Spider' Member 'Tylerb' Pleads Guilty
articles.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior Scattered Spider member, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major tech companies and stole tens of millions in cryptocurrency. Buchanan faces over 20 years in prison and is the first major member of the prolific social-engineering group to be convicted.

articles.title: QIMMA قِمّة ⛰: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
articles.summary: TIIUAE launches QIMMA, a leaderboard for evaluating Arabic LLMs with emphasis on quality metrics and benchmarks tailored to Arabic language tasks.

articles.title: AI and the Future of Cybersecurity: Why Openness Matters
articles.summary: Examines the role of open-source and transparent AI models in strengthening cybersecurity defenses. Argues that openness in AI development enables better threat detection and collaborative security research.

articles.title: Maximizing Memory Efficiency to Run Bigger Models on NVIDIA Jetson
articles.summary: NVIDIA Jetson techniques enable deployment of multi-billion-parameter generative AI models on edge devices with constrained memory. The approach targets physical AI agents and autonomous robots that require on-device inference.

articles.title: Run High-Throughput Reinforcement Learning Training with End-to-End FP8 Precision
articles.summary: End-to-end FP8 precision enables high-throughput reinforcement learning training for reasoning-grade LLMs using algorithms like GRPO. The approach reduces memory and compute overhead for the bifurcated RL training pipeline.

articles.title: Accelerate Generative AI Inference on Amazon SageMaker AI with G7e Instances
articles.summary: AWS announces G7e instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs on SageMaker, offering 96 GB GDDR7 memory per GPU and up to 2.3x inference performance over G6e. Single nodes can host 35B parameter models in FP16, while 8-GPU configurations support 300B parameter models, enabling cost-effective deployment of large open-source foundation models.

articles.title: Changes to GitHub Copilot Individual plans
articles.summary: GitHub pauses new Copilot Pro/Pro+/Student sign-ups and tightens usage limits to manage agentic workload demands, with Pro+ offering 5X higher limits than Pro and Opus models removed from Pro tier. Usage limits are now displayed in VS Code and Copilot CLI to help users avoid hitting guardrails designed to maintain service reliability.

articles.title: ToolSimulator: scalable tool testing for AI agents
articles.summary: ToolSimulator, part of Strands Evals SDK, provides LLM-powered tool simulation for testing AI agents without live API calls or static mocks. It supports stateful multi-turn workflows, Pydantic schema enforcement, and edge-case validation, enabling safe evaluation of agent tool-use before production deployment.

articles.title: Mitigating Indirect AGENTS.md Injection Attacks in Agentic Environments
articles.summary: The post addresses injection attack vulnerabilities in AI coding agents like OpenAI Codex that execute tasks, generate code, and create pull requests. It covers mitigation strategies for indirect prompt injection attacks targeting agentic systems in development workflows.

articles.title: Highlights from Git 2.54
articles.summary: Git 2.54 introduces git history, an experimental command for simpler history rewrites supporting reword and split operations without the complexity of interactive rebase. The release includes contributions from 137 developers, 66 of them new, and covers features from both 2.53 and 2.54 versions.

articles.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: Claude Opus 4.7 in Amazon Bedrock, AWS Interconnect GA, and more (April 20, 2026)
articles.summary: Claude Opus 4.7 is now available in Amazon Bedrock with improved agentic coding performance (64.3% on SWE-bench Pro, 87.6% on SWE-bench Verified) and knowledge work capabilities. Bedrock's next-generation inference engine adds dynamic capacity allocation and adaptive thinking token budgeting for long-running tasks.

articles.title: Omnichannel ordering with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
articles.summary: AWS demonstrates building a voice-enabled omnichannel ordering system using Bedrock AgentCore and Nova 2 Sonic, handling bidirectional audio streams and multi-turn context across mobile, web, and voice interfaces. The solution uses AWS CDK for infrastructure and Strands for agent orchestration, with modular components for backend integration.

articles.title: From public static void main to Golden Kubestronaut: The Art of unlearning
articles.summary: A developer's personal account of transitioning from traditional Java development to cloud-native architecture, holding all five Kubernetes certifications and achieving CNCF Golden Kubestronaut status. The piece emphasizes that the core challenge isn't learning new tools but unlearning traditional infrastructure assumptions—moving from siloed development to owning operational concerns like pod failures and YAML configuration.

articles.title: Full-Stack Optimizations for Agentic Inference with NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo optimizes inference for coding agents at production scale: Stripe generates 1,300+ PRs/week, Ramp 30% of merged PRs, Spotify 650+ PRs/month. The stack reduces latency for agents making hundreds of API calls per session with full conversation history.

articles.title: Build a More Secure, Always-On Local AI Agent with OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw
articles.summary: NemoClaw and OpenClaw enable long-running local AI agents that perform file operations, API calls, and multi-step workflows with security and privacy benefits of on-device execution. The approach shifts agents from stateless Q&A systems to persistent autonomous assistants.

articles.title: Building an emoji list generator with the GitHub Copilot CLI
articles.summary: GitHub demonstrates Copilot CLI capabilities by building an emoji list generator using the Copilot SDK, opentui/core for terminal UI, and clipboardy for clipboard access. The project runs in the terminal and uses Copilot's plan mode to convert user input into relevant emoji selections.

articles.title: Bringing more transparency to GitHub's status page
articles.summary: GitHub adds a new 'Degraded Performance' incident severity level between Partial Outage and Major Outage, publishes per-service uptime metrics, and introduces a dedicated 'Copilot AI Model Providers' component for clearer visibility into model provider availability. These changes improve incident classification accuracy and communication specificity during service disruptions.

articles.title: Accelerate Clean, Modular, Nuclear Reactor Design with AI Physics
articles.summary: NVIDIA's AI physics approach accelerates design of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV nuclear designs by balancing safety, efficiency, and economics. The work applies physics-informed AI to standardize reactor designs and shift construction to controlled manufacturing.

articles.title: Advancing secret sync with workload identity federation
articles.summary: Vault Enterprise 2.0 adds workload identity federation support to secret sync, replacing long-lived static cloud credentials with short-lived federated identity tokens for AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, and Google Secret Manager. This eliminates the need to store IAM access keys, service principal secrets, or service account keys, aligning secret distribution with cloud-native identity-first security models.

articles.title: K3s on On-Prem Infrastructures the GitOps Way: Writing a Custom k0rdent Template from Scratch
articles.summary: Practical walkthrough of provisioning K3s clusters on Proxmox using k0rdent's Bring Your Own Template (BYOT) approach with custom Helm charts. Replaces manual VM creation and bash scripts with declarative, repeatable cluster lifecycle management on on-premise infrastructure.

articles.title: The AI-driven shift in vulnerability discovery: What maintainers and bug finders need to know
articles.summary: Examines how AI models have accelerated vulnerability discovery in open-source software, enabling non-experts to find real flaws with minimal effort while also generating convincing false positives. Warns that OSS maintainers face an incoming surge of patch requests and that downstream release/compliance systems will face strain as fixes proliferate.

articles.title: Why MicroVMs: The Architecture Behind Docker Sandboxes
articles.summary: Docker Sandboxes uses microVMs to isolate AI coding agents, avoiding the trade-offs of full VMs (slow cold starts), containers (Docker-in-Docker privilege escalation), and WASM isolates (V8 hardening challenges). MicroVMs provide ephemeral, session-heavy agent workflows with real OS-level isolation and native Docker support.

articles.title: How GitHub uses eBPF to improve deployment safety
articles.summary: GitHub uses eBPF to selectively monitor and block circular dependencies in deployment scripts, preventing deployment code from inadvertently calling internal services or downloading binaries from github.com when the platform is down. The approach mitigates the risk of deployment failures cascading into platform outages.

articles.title: How to Build Vision AI Pipelines Using NVIDIA DeepStream Coding Agents
articles.summary: DeepStream 9 integrates coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor) to auto-generate optimized real-time vision AI pipelines, reducing development friction from complex data pipelines and lengthy coding cycles. The approach targets deployment of production vision applications.

articles.title: Introducing Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 model in Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Claude Opus 4.7 launches in Amazon Bedrock with Bedrock's next-generation inference engine featuring dynamic capacity allocation and zero operator access (prompts never visible to AWS/Anthropic operators). Benchmarks: 64.3% SWE-bench Pro, 87.6% SWE-bench Verified, 69.4% Terminal-Bench 2.0 for agentic coding; improved document creation and financial analysis for knowledge work.

articles.title: Designing synthetic datasets for the real world: Mechanism design and reasoning from first principles
articles.summary: Google Research explores principled methods for generating synthetic datasets using mechanism design theory and first-principles reasoning. The work addresses the gap between synthetic data quality and real-world applicability for training AI models.

articles.title: AI-generated synthetic neurons speed up brain mapping
articles.summary: Google Research applies generative models to accelerate neuroscience research by synthesizing realistic neuron data. The approach reduces the computational burden of brain mapping by generating high-fidelity synthetic training data.

articles.title: How To Measure the ROI of Developer Tools
articles.summary: Discusses frameworks for measuring return on investment in developer tools and platform engineering initiatives, covering internal surveys, productivity metrics, and cost-benefit analysis. Addresses how ROI measurement differs by team size and growth stage.

articles.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
articles.summary: Introduces Ecom-RLVE, a benchmark environment for training and evaluating e-commerce agents with reinforcement learning and verifiable execution. Enables agents to handle multi-turn conversations with product search, comparison, and transaction capabilities.

articles.title: Training and Finetuning Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Tutorial on training multimodal embeddings and rerankers using Sentence Transformers library with text and image inputs. Covers loss functions, data preparation, and evaluation metrics for cross-modal retrieval tasks.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: Announces tooling to automatically convert Hugging Face Transformers models to MLX format for Apple Silicon inference. Streamlines model porting for on-device deployment without manual optimization work.

articles.title: Build a personal organization command center with GitHub Copilot CLI
articles.summary: A GitHub staff engineer built a personal command center using the Copilot CLI to unify scattered tools into a single interface, moving from idea to working prototype in a day using AI-assisted planning and implementation. The project demonstrates practical productivity gains from agentic tool-use.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS introduces granular audio tags for precise control over expressive speech synthesis. The model enables fine-grained direction of AI-generated audio output through structured tagging mechanisms.

articles.title: Agentic AI changes the shape of trust
articles.summary: HashiCorp identifies a gap in identity models for agentic AI: when agents assume roles and complete tasks, access paths don't close cleanly, credentials get cached, and roles stay warm, creating quiet accumulation of unrevoked access that traditional IAM cannot track. The post argues that identity systems built around people and discrete login events fail to account for continuous agent activity and implicit authority delegation.

articles.title: Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
articles.summary: Analyzes the VAKRA benchmark for evaluating agent reasoning and tool-use capabilities, including systematic failure mode analysis. Provides insights into how agents handle complex multi-step tasks and error recovery.

articles.title: AWS Interconnect is now generally available, with a new option to simplify last-mile connectivity
articles.summary: AWS Interconnect GA adds managed private connectivity for multicloud and hybrid environments, with new last-mile capability for branch offices and data centers. Simplifies VPC-to-VPC connections across cloud providers and eliminates manual VPN/colocation coordination through AWS Console configuration.

articles.title: Why We Chose the Harder Path: Docker Hardened Images, One Year Later
articles.summary: Docker Hardened Images (DHI) crossed 500k daily pulls and 25k continuously patched OS artifacts in a SLSA Build Level 3 pipeline, with the catalog growing to 2,000+ hardened images, MCP servers, and Helm charts. The program prioritizes free, open-source multi-distro hardening with signed attestations and source-level package builds rather than proprietary solutions, running over a million builds regularly for CVE and distro patching.

articles.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
articles.summary: Microsoft released 167 security patches including a SharePoint Server zero-day (CVE-2026-32201) enabling spoofing attacks and BlueHammer (CVE-2026-33825), a Windows Defender privilege escalation flaw with public exploit code. Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and Adobe Reader patched an actively exploited RCE vulnerability.

articles.title: Building Custom Atomistic Simulation Workflows for Chemistry and Materials Science with NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit
articles.summary: ALCHEMI Toolkit bridges the accuracy-speed tradeoff in computational chemistry by combining high-fidelity ab initio methods (DFT) with fast classical force fields. The toolkit enables researchers to build custom atomistic workflows beyond the few-hundred-atom limit of traditional DFT.

articles.title: NVIDIA NVbandwidth: Your Essential Tool for Measuring GPU Interconnect and Memory Performance
articles.summary: NVbandwidth is a profiling tool for measuring GPU memory and interconnect performance in CUDA applications, supporting both single-GPU and multi-GPU systems. The tool helps developers understand data transfer bottlenecks critical to application performance.

articles.title: NVIDIA Ising Introduces AI-Powered Workflows to Build Fault-Tolerant Quantum Systems
articles.summary: NVIDIA Ising launches open AI models for quantum processor design across two domains: Ising Calibration and Ising Decoding, targeting qubit noise mitigation where current quantum processors error roughly once per thousand operations. The models enable fault-tolerant quantum system construction.

articles.title: Vault Enterprise 2.0 modernizes identity security at scale
articles.summary: Vault Enterprise 2.0 GA introduces workload identity federation for eliminating static credentials, expanded Linux credential rotation, and envelope encryption for streaming workloads. The release includes enhanced Terraform/Kubernetes integrations, a redesigned UI, and transitions to a new versioning model aligned with IBM practices (jumping from v1.21 to v2.0.0).

articles.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: Claude Mythos Preview in Amazon Bedrock, AWS Agent Registry, and more (April 13, 2026)
articles.summary: Claude Mythos, Anthropic's new cybersecurity-focused model, is now available as a gated preview on Amazon Bedrock. Amazon Bedrock adds IAM principal cost allocation tagging for tracking model inference spending by team or cost center, flowing into Cost Explorer and detailed usage reports.

articles.title: How to Analyze Hugging Face for Arm64 Readiness
articles.summary: Docker and Arm collaborated on a 7-tool MCP chain that systematically diagnoses Arm64 compatibility issues in Hugging Face Spaces, identifying that roughly 80% fail due to hardcoded dependency URLs (e.g., flash-attn wheels pinned to linux_x86_64) rather than code problems. The guide walks through the ACE-Step v1.5 music generation model failure and how the chain surfaces blockers automatically in about 15 minutes.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 improves spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding for autonomous robotics applications. The model enhances embodied reasoning capabilities to handle real-world robotic tasks with better environmental comprehension.

articles.title: ingress-nginx to Envoy Gateway migration on CNCF internal services cluster
articles.summary: Documents CNCF's migration from ingress-nginx to Envoy Gateway following ingress-nginx retirement. Compares architectures: ingress-nginx uses a single LoadBalancer service while Gateway API allows per-route gateways, reducing cloud load balancer costs by consolidating to a shared Gateway object across multiple HTTPRoutes.

articles.title: MiniMax M2.7 Advances Scalable Agentic Workflows on NVIDIA Platforms for Complex AI Applications
articles.summary: MiniMax M2.7 extends the M2.5 model with improvements for agentic workflows, reasoning, ML research, and software engineering tasks using sparse mixture-of-experts architecture. The open-weights release is available through NVIDIA and the open-source inference ecosystem.

articles.title: ConvApparel: Measuring and bridging the realism gap in user simulators
articles.summary: Google Research introduces ConvApparel, a framework for evaluating and improving the realism of user simulators in conversational AI systems. The work quantifies the gap between simulated and real user behavior, then proposes methods to close it.

articles.title: Simplifying Terraform dynamic credentials on AWS with native OIDC integration
articles.summary: AWS Account Factory for Terraform (AFT) now supports native OIDC integration (terraform_oidc_integration = true), automatically establishing trust relationships between AWS and HCP Terraform workspaces without manual IAM role, trust policy, or environment variable configuration. This abstracts credential federation into the AFT workflow, eliminating operational overhead and misconfiguration risk for platform teams standardizing secure multi-account provisioning.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Introduces multimodal embedding and reranker models in Sentence Transformers supporting text-image pairs. Enables cross-modal retrieval and ranking for vision-language applications.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Release of Waypoint-1.5, an interactive 3D environment simulator optimized for consumer GPUs. Enables training of embodied agents and multimodal models with improved visual fidelity and reduced compute requirements.

articles.title: Improving the academic workflow: Introducing two AI agents for better figures and peer review
articles.summary: Google introduces two AI agents designed to streamline academic workflows: one for generating publication-quality figures and another for assisting with peer review. The agents leverage LLM capabilities to automate repetitive tasks in academic publishing.

articles.title: Reclaim Developer Hours through Smarter Vulnerability Prioritization with Docker and Mend.io
articles.summary: Mend.io integrated with Docker Hardened Images to automatically distinguish base image vulnerabilities from application-layer risks using VEX statements, filtering out non-exploitable CVEs. The integration provides zero-configuration setup with automatic DHI detection, visual indicators in the Mend UI, and dynamic risk triage combining risk factors and reachability analysis.

articles.title: Safetensors is Joining the PyTorch Foundation
articles.summary: Safetensors, the safe serialization format for ML model weights, is now a PyTorch Foundation project. This move formalizes the format's role in the ecosystem and ensures long-term governance and maintenance for a tool widely adopted across model distribution and inference.

articles.title: Launching S3 Files, making S3 buckets accessible as file systems
articles.summary: Amazon S3 Files provides POSIX-compatible file system access to S3 buckets, enabling in-place edits and automatic synchronization with S3 objects. Multiple compute resources can attach to the same bucket for data sharing without duplication, bridging the object-storage-vs-file-system tradeoff.

articles.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
articles.summary: Russian military intelligence (Forest Blizzard/APT28) exploited known vulnerabilities in end-of-life routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks and 200+ organizations without deploying malware. The campaign used DNS redirection at the router level to intercept credentials at scale.

articles.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: AWS DevOps Agent & Security Agent GA, Product Lifecycle updates, and more (April 6, 2026)
articles.summary: AWS DevOps Agent and Security Agent reach GA after preview, offering autonomous multi-step incident response and continuous penetration testing. Preview customers report up to 75% lower MTTR, 3-5x faster resolution, and 50%+ faster vulnerability detection at scale.

articles.title: Germany Doxes "UNKN," Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
articles.summary: German authorities identified Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin as UNKN, the operator behind GandCrab and REvil ransomware groups responsible for 130+ attacks extracting nearly 2 million euros from victims with 35+ million euros in total damage. Shchukin pioneered double-extortion tactics and was previously named in a 2023 U.S. Justice Department cryptocurrency seizure filing.

articles.title: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails supports cross-account safeguards with centralized control and management
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails GA adds cross-account enforcement via organization-level policies, automatically applying safety controls across all member accounts and OUs. Enables centralized management of model invocation safeguards with flexibility for account-level and application-specific overrides.
